session_summary: Deployed app to EC2 (fixed IPv6→IPv4 DB connection via Supabase pooler). Built in-app feedback collection (TASK-015). Hardened deploy.sh security. Added Agent Conduct Rules and Task Lifecycle gates to CLAUDE.md. Collected first round of real user feedback — actionable items below.

workflow_position.step_reached: User feedback collected. Next agent needs to CREATE new tasks from the feedback below, then go through the full Task Lifecycle (PICK → PLAN → approve → EXECUTE → CODE REVIEW → PR → CLOSE) for each.
workflow_position.next_action: Create TASK-016 (markdown rendering + quick reply buttons) and TASK-017 (system prompt iteration). These may overlap with TASK-008 (structured output) and TASK-009 (UI transformation) from the original plan — coordinate accordingly.

git_state.branching_strategy: per-task — each task gets its own branch off main, merges back via PR when done

deployed.infra: EC2 (107.23.228.4) + nginx + PM2 + Supabase (connection pooler on port 6543)
deployed.secrets: AWS SSM Parameter Store under /unpack/ (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, DATABASE_URL, VITE_SUPABASE_URL, VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY)

task_status.TASK-007: planned — user testing + prompt iteration (NOW UNBLOCKED, feedback collected below)
task_status.TASK-009: planned — UI transformation (stepper, cards, slide preview)

user_feedback.source: First round of real user testing (2026-03-05)
raw_feedback: Add some basic markdown formatting. The AI keeps outputting **bold** with raw asterisks showing.

Can you have stubs at the top of the chat so that the user only needs to select what is next, not type. But in short really like it. 2 points: 1. md formatting and 2. add stubs above the chat so I can just click when there are options rather than typing.
Whatever you can do to make it punchier and reduce the time taken to develop. Some questions felt a little repetitive.

I feel like this isn't particularly useful for me and I'll just make my own slides. Two ways it's good: it asked 2 useful concrete questions: 1. who is your audience, and 2. give me a concrete example of [CLAIM]. It was very insistent I had to find a problem to solve because I was presenting to engineers.
The vibe feels like I'm getting interviewed, not that we're working cooperatively towards a solution.

actionable_items.issue: Markdown not rendered — raw **bold**, *italic* showing as plaintext
actionable_items.fix: Add a markdown renderer (react-markdown or similar) to message display

actionable_items.issue: No quick reply buttons — users have to type everything
actionable_items.fix: Add suggested response chips/buttons above the input when the bot asks questions with clear options

actionable_items.issue: Too many questions, feels repetitive and like an interrogation
actionable_items.fix: Reduce question count in system prompt. Combine related questions. Move faster through context gathering.

actionable_items.issue: Too insistent on problem framing — not every presentation is problem→solution
actionable_items.fix: System prompt should recognize presentations that don't fit problem/solution (demos, show-and-tell, updates) and adapt structure accordingly

actionable_items.issue: Vibe is 'interview' not 'collaboration' — bot asks too much, gives too little
actionable_items.fix: Prompt should instruct bot to offer suggestions, examples, and partial drafts earlier. Give before you ask. Show the user you're building WITH them.

TASK-016.title: Markdown rendering + quick reply buttons
TASK-016.description: Render bot messages with markdown (bold, italic, lists, headers). Add quick reply suggestion buttons above the input when the bot asks questions with clear options (e.g., presentation type, audience). This addresses FB-1 and FB-2 from user feedback.
TASK-016.notes: This overlaps with TASK-009 (UI transformation). Consider whether to fold quick replies into TASK-009 or keep separate. Markdown rendering is standalone and should ship first regardless.

TASK-017.title: System prompt iteration based on user feedback
TASK-017.description: Address FB-3 (repetitive questions), FB-4 (forced problem framing), and FB-5 (interview vibe). Make the bot more collaborative: offer suggestions early, reduce question count, recognize non-problem/solution presentations, give before you ask.
TASK-017.notes: This IS TASK-007 (user testing + prompt iteration) from the original plan. Consider merging or replacing TASK-007 with this.

task_009_overlap: TASK-009 (UI transformation) includes stepper, cards, and slide preview. Quick reply buttons (FB-2) are a natural fit for TASK-009's card-based message rendering. However, TASK-009 depends on TASK-008 (structured output). Markdown rendering does NOT depend on structured output and should ship independently. Recommended approach: ship markdown rendering now in TASK-016, defer quick replies to TASK-009 if structured output is needed, OR ship quick replies as a simple heuristic (detect question marks in bot messages) without structured output.

important_rules.task_lifecycle: CLAUDE.md now has a mandatory Task Lifecycle section with explicit gates. Every task: PICK → PLAN (stop, wait for approval) → MODE → EXECUTE → CODE REVIEW (gate) → PR (gate) → CLOSE. Never skip gates.
important_rules.agent_conduct: Verify third-party UI instructions against current docs. Surface all security-critical artifacts. Security checklist on infra tasks.
important_rules.deploy_after_merge: After every PR merge, redeploy to EC2: ssh -i ~/.ssh/unpack-key.pem ec2-user@107.23.228.4 'cd ~/unpack && bash deploy.sh'
